description:
You are acting as a QA engineer who must prepare a repeatable Python-based test environment for an upcoming automated regression suite.  
    Your goal is to construct **exactly** the directory tree, files, and content described below inside your Linux home directory **/home/user**.  
    The automated grader will inspect every byte, so please follow the specification literally (capitalisation, spacing, line-breaks and file permissions all matter).

    1. Directory layout  
       • /home/user/qa_env/  
         ├── configs/  
         ├── logs/  
         └── venv/          (a Python 3 virtual-environment that you will create)  

    2. Configuration file  
       Path: /home/user/qa_env/configs/app_config.ini  
       EXACT contents (include the blank line between the two sections!):  
       ```
       [DEFAULT]
       env = staging
       version = 1.0.0

       [database]
       host = localhost
       port = 5432
       user = qa_user
       password = qa_pass
       ```

    3. Environment-initialisation script  
       Path: /home/user/qa_env/env.sh  
       It must be an **executable** shell script (`chmod +x`).  
       EXACT contents (no extra spaces after the quotes):  
       ```
       #!/usr/bin/env bash
       export QA_ENV_ROOT="/home/user/qa_env"
       export QA_ENV="staging"
       export DB_HOST="localhost"
       export DB_PORT="5432"
       export DB_USER="qa_user"
       export DB_PASSWORD="qa_pass"
       ```

    4. Python virtual environment  
       • Create it with Python 3 under: /home/user/qa_env/venv  
       • Inside that venv install the two packages listed below at the precise pinned versions.  
         After installation, `pip list` executed **inside** the venv must at minimum show:  
         - requests 2.31.0  
         - pytest   7.4.0  

    5. Requirements file  
       Path: /home/user/qa_env/requirements.txt  
       EXACT contents:  
       ```
       requests==2.31.0
       pytest==7.4.0
       ```

    6. Audit log  
       Path: /home/user/qa_env/logs/setup_audit.log  
       The file must contain exactly five lines, each line in the format  
       `<ISO-8601 timestamp> <step> OK`  
       Use the **fixed timestamp** 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z for every line so automated checks are deterministic.  
       The final file must therefore be:  
       ```
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_dirs OK
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_config OK
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_env_script OK
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_virtualenv OK
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z install_requirements OK
       ```

    7. Completion marker  
       Path: /home/user/qa_env/.setup_complete  
       The file must consist of the single text string:  
       ```
       QA_ENV_READY
       ```

    Summary of what will be verified:  
    • The directory tree and all paths exist exactly as written.  
    • The files app_config.ini, env.sh, requirements.txt, setup_audit.log and .setup_complete exist and match the *exact* byte-for-byte content stated above.  
    • env.sh is executable.  
    • A valid Python 3 virtual environment lives in /home/user/qa_env/venv.  
    • Inside that venv, packages requests 2.31.0 and pytest 7.4.0 are installed.  

    Produce any intermediate console output you find helpful, but ensure the final filesystem state matches this specification.
truth:
<!-- Directory & file existence -->
    - Directories that must exist:
      1. /home/user/qa_env
      2. /home/user/qa_env/configs
      3. /home/user/qa_env/logs
      4. /home/user/qa_env/venv  (created via python -m venv)

    <!-- app_config.ini -->
    Path: /home/user/qa_env/configs/app_config.ini
    Content bytes (including trailing newline):
    [DEFAULT]\n
    env = staging\n
    version = 1.0.0\n
    \n
    [database]\n
    host = localhost\n
    port = 5432\n
    user = qa_user\n
    password = qa_pass\n

    <!-- env.sh -->
    Path: /home/user/qa_env/env.sh
    Permissions must include execute bit for owner (0755 or 0700 acceptable).
    Content bytes:
    #!/usr/bin/env bash\n
    export QA_ENV_ROOT="/home/user/qa_env"\n
    export QA_ENV="staging"\n
    export DB_HOST="localhost"\n
    export DB_PORT="5432"\n
    export DB_USER="qa_user"\n
    export DB_PASSWORD="qa_pass"\n

    <!-- requirements.txt -->
    Path: /home/user/qa_env/requirements.txt
    Content bytes:
    requests==2.31.0\n
    pytest==7.4.0\n

    <!-- venv -->
    - /home/user/qa_env/venv/bin/activate must exist.
    - /home/user/qa_env/venv/bin/python must be an executable ELF/py launcher.
    - Running "/home/user/qa_env/venv/bin/pip list" should list at least:
      requests  2.31.0
      pytest    7.4.0

    <!-- log file -->
    Path: /home/user/qa_env/logs/setup_audit.log
    Content bytes:
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_dirs OK\n
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_config OK\n
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_env_script OK\n
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z create_virtualenv OK\n
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z install_requirements OK\n

    <!-- completion marker -->
    Path: /home/user/qa_env/.setup_complete
    Content bytes:
    QA_ENV_READY\n
